title: Semiconducting Polymers Post‐Functionalized With Ureido‐Pyrimidinone for Robust Stretchable Transistors
primary_category: 可穿戴健康与生理监测
categories: 可穿戴健康与生理监测
pdf_version_label: 暂无可合法自动获取的开放全文

abstract.original: ABSTRACT Stretchable polymer semiconductors promise transformative wearable electronics but face a stretchability‐mobility trade‐off. Proven solutions include incorporating long alkyl side chains as flexible segments or, more effectively, introducing hydrogen‐bonding motifs as dynamic cross‐links that enhance stretchability via energy dissipation and polymer network reorganization under strain while maintaining charge‐carrier mobility. Embedding quadruple hydrogen‐bonding units into conjugated polymers (CPs) side chains or backbones fosters supramolecular network formation, which enhances mechanical robustness but demands meticulous molecular design. This work employs a post‑functionalization strategy to graft ureido‐pyrimidinone (UPy) units onto diketopyrrolopyrrole‑based CPs (PDPP2T) containing (6‑bromohexyl)oxy( tert ‑butyl)dimethylsilane (OTBS) linkers, generating PDPP2T‐UPy‐x polymers with 0, 2, and 5 mol% UPy (D0, D2, and D5). This design introduces two hydrogen‐bonding moieties along extended alkyl chains, namely, weaker mid‐chain urethane linkages and strong end‐chain quadruple UPy units. Devices based on D5 retain mobilities within the same order of magnitude as initial values (3.02–1.12 × 10 −1 cm 2 V −1 s −1 ) after 500 stretching cycles at 100% strain. This performance outperforms typical intrinsically stretchable semiconducting polymers through post‐synthetic tuning of hierarchical hydrogen‐bond density without disrupting the conjugated backbone, thereby safeguarding charge transport while facilitating energy dissipation for superior mechanical endurance. Beyond optimizing electrical‐mechanical properties, this strategy offers broad applicability to other CPs.
